ops.title: jj workspace add dirs have .jj but no .git — git-dependent tools (cc-notes CLI, prek/pre-commit) fail there
ops.body:
Directories created by `jj workspace add` are jj-only (no colocated `.git`), even when the primary repo is git-colocated. Tools that require a git repository fail inside them: the cc-notes CLI errors with "open git repository ... does not exist", and prek/pre-commit refuses to run.

**Why:** jj only colocates `.git` in the default workspace; secondary workspaces get a `.jj` pointer alone.

**How to apply:** run refs-only operations (cc-notes task/doc commands, git tag) from the primary repo — they touch refs, not the working tree, so this is safe even with parallel sessions; for commit hooks, either invoke the underlying linter binary with the repo config directly, or colocate a throwaway git checkout. Related: [[parallel-sessions-own-the-working-copy]].
